docs.title: Dell
docs.text:
pnputil /add-driver "C:\Users\Administrator\<model>\*.inf" /subdirs /install

⚠️ Folder name changes per model. Replace <model> with folder name.

docs.title: Lenovo
docs.text: pnputil /add-driver "C:\DRIVERS\*.inf" /subdirs /install

docs.title: HP
docs.text:
pnputil /add-driver "C:\SWSetup\*.inf" /subdirs /install

Author: Joel Skerman | Date: 23 Apr 2026

docs.title: Useful Links

docs.title: Installing Drivers from a .cab File Using PowerShell (Audit Mode)
docs.text: This guide explains how to extract and install drivers from a .cab file located in the Downloads folder while in Audit Mode. - ⚠️ these pre-made commands are specifically for the Panasonic FZ-G1

docs.title: One-Liner (Extract + Install)
docs.text:
$cab="C:\Users\Administrator\Downloads\G1F_Mk2_Win10x64_1803_V1.00.cab"
$out="C:\Drivers\Extracted"
mkdir $out -Force | Out-Null
expand -F:* $cab $out
pnputil /add-driver "$out\*.inf" /subdirs /install
  • ⚠️ Replace G1F_Mk2_Win10x64_1803_V1.00.cab with the filename of the CAB file you downloaded
    • ⚠️ you can do this more easily by pasting into notepad.exe and editing there

docs.title: Explanation:
docs.text:
• /add-driver → Adds drivers to the Windows driver store
  • /subdirs → Searches all subfolders for .inf files
  • /install → Installs drivers if matching hardware is found

docs.title: ⚠️ Important Notes
docs.text:
• Run PowerShell as Administrator
  • If drivers do not install:
  • The hardware may not be connected
  • Drivers may already be installed

docs.title: Optional — List Installed Drivers
docs.text:
pnputil /enum-drivers

Author: Joel Skerman | Date: 27 Apr 2026 | Updated 28 Apr 2026

docs.title: Driver Installation Guide
docs.text: ⚠️ This guide assumes that you are using audit mode, accessible by pressing ctrl+shift+f3 in windows OOBE (Out Of Box Experience)

docs.title: Step 1 – Open Driver Resource Page
docs.text:
Go to the appropriate driver download page:

  • Dell Driver Packs
  • HP Driver Packs
  • Lenovo Driver Packs

docs.title: Step 2 – Find Your Device & Download Drivers!
docs.text:
• Search for your exact model
  • Download the driver pack (.exe) notes:
    • ⚠️ Dell Driver Packs require you to select the "Line Of Business" before searching for the specific model eg: XPS or Latitude
    • ⚠️ Lenovo puts their most recent driver packages at the bottom of the page
    • ⚠️ It is best to use Machine Type to find Lenovo driver packs
    • ⚠️ Use ctrl+f to find laptop models for Dell and HP

docs.title: Step 3 – Extract the Driver Package
docs.text:
Run the .exe file you just downloaded to extract drivers.

  • ⚠️ This does not install drivers yet

docs.title: Step 4 – Install Drivers
docs.text: Open Powershell as Administrator. - ⚠️ If you are in audit mode you can do this by pressing win+r and typing powershell

docs.title: Dell
docs.text:
pnputil /add-driver "C:\Users\Administrator\<model>\*.inf" /subdirs /install

⚠️ Folder name changes per model. Replace <model> with folder name.

docs.title: Lenovo
docs.text: pnputil /add-driver "C:\DRIVERS\*.inf" /subdirs /install

docs.title: HP
docs.text: pnputil /add-driver "C:\SWSetup\*.inf" /subdirs /install

docs.title: ⚠️ Troubleshooting
docs.text:
If these commands do not work verify the driver folder exists at one of these locations

Brand Location Dell C:\Users\<username>\<model>\ HP C:\SWSetup\ Lenovo C:\DRIVERS\

docs.title: Step 5 – Wait for Installation
docs.text: Drivers will install automatically. Be patient this can take a while

docs.title: Step 6 – Restart
docs.text: Restart the computer after installation if prompted.

docs.title: Step 7 – Run Windows Update
docs.text: stop blancco from continuing (if it is running) and run regular windows update once

docs.title: Step 8 – Reboot
docs.text:
Reboot system and let blancco run! Now that you have read the documentation you can find a minified version of this page at 9dtr.com/w

Author: Joel Skerman | Date: 23 Apr 2026 | Updated 27 Apr 2026

docs.title: Step 1
docs.text: Install Windows 10 from a USB

docs.title: Step 2
docs.text: Enter Audit Mode by pressing ctrl+shift+f3

docs.title: Step 3
docs.text: Disable Microsoft Serial Ballpoint in device manager - ⚠️ Note: Pressing win+x brings up a menu where you can click on device manager

docs.title: Step 4
docs.text:
• Download this file Download
  • After Download Is complete run the following in powershell as Administrator
$cab="C:\Users\Administrator\Downloads\G1F_Mk2_Win10x64_1803_V1.00.cab"
$out="C:\Drivers\FZ-G1"
mkdir $out -Force | Out-Null
expand -F:* $cab $out
pnputil /add-driver "$out\*.inf" /subdirs /install
  • ⚠️ Reboot after complete

If you need to rerun use the following:

docs.title: Step 5
docs.text:
Run Windows update and reboot if prompted

Author: Joel Skerman | Date: 28 Apr 2026
